MuddingExperience.brief: Constants representing various player experience categories within the Mudding system.
MuddingExperience.details: The MuddingExperience group encapsulates constants that define different experience level classifications used in the Mudding environment. These constants distinguish between newly created players, legacy players, and older characters, enabling consistent handling, display, and logic application based on player experience status. By centralizing these related constants, the group facilitates maintainability and clarity in managing experience-related data across the system.
MuddingExperience.notes: The constants are organized to support differentiation between new and legacy players, ensuring backward compatibility and smooth system evolution.
MuddingExperience.rationale: This group exists to provide a clear and maintainable way to categorize player experience levels, supporting game logic, compatibility, and system consistency by grouping related constants together.
MuddingExperience.name: MuddingExperience

MEXP_TOTAL_NEWBIE.brief: The constant representing the initial experience points assigned to new players.
MEXP_TOTAL_NEWBIE.details: MuddingExperience::MEXP_TOTAL_NEWBIE is a constant that defines the starting experience points for players when they begin their journey in the game. It is set to zero, indicating that new players start without any accumulated experience. This value is used during player creation and initialization processes, particularly when setting the 'mud_exp' attribute in a player's data structure. Its consistent use ensures that all new characters begin with a standardized experience level, facilitating straightforward onboarding and progression. The constant is referenced in input handling functions, such as when a player opts to start as a new character, to reliably assign the correct initial experience value.
MEXP_TOTAL_NEWBIE.notes: Since the value is a compile-time constant, it ensures that the initial experience remains consistent across all instances and can be optimized by the compiler. This pattern simplifies the initialization logic and maintains uniformity for new player characters.
MEXP_TOTAL_NEWBIE.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to assign the starting experience points to new players, supporting consistent game behavior and simplifying future adjustments to the initial experience value if needed.
MEXP_TOTAL_NEWBIE.name: MEXP_TOTAL_NEWBIE

usages.structconn_1_1_get_mud_exp_state, State: Constant value assigned to ch->pcdata->mud_exp when the user selects option '1'.

MEXP_LEGACY_NEWBIE.brief: Constant representing the experience level assigned to legacy new players in the Mudding system.
MEXP_LEGACY_NEWBIE.details: The constant `MEXP_LEGACY_NEWBIE` is defined with a value of 1 and signifies the experience level for legacy new players within the Mudding environment. It is used during input handling to set a character's `mud_exp` field when the player selects an option indicating their status as a new or inexperienced player, such as choosing option '2'. This value helps the system identify and differentiate legacy newcomers from other players, enabling tailored gameplay mechanics or experience adjustments based on player experience level.
MEXP_LEGACY_NEWBIE.notes: Typically stored in the character's `mud_exp` attribute, this constant is part of a set of predefined experience level identifiers used throughout the codebase to manage player progression and status.
MEXP_LEGACY_NEWBIE.rationale: The constant exists to standardize the representation of legacy new players' experience level, ensuring consistent assignment and recognition across different parts of the system, and supporting game logic that depends on player experience status.
MEXP_LEGACY_NEWBIE.name: MEXP_LEGACY_NEWBIE

usages.structconn_1_1_get_mud_exp_state, State: Constant value assigned to ch->pcdata->mud_exp when the user selects option '2'.

MEXP_LEGACY_OLDBIE.brief: A constant representing the legacy experience type for older characters in the Mudding system.
MEXP_LEGACY_OLDBIE.details: The constant MEXP_LEGACY_OLDBIE is used to identify and differentiate the legacy experience category within the Mudding experience system. Initialized with the value 2, it is assigned to character data fields such as 'mud_exp' during character creation or gameplay when a player selects the legacy experience option. This constant ensures that older or legacy experience profiles are correctly recognized and handled by the system, maintaining compatibility and appropriate behavior for characters with legacy experience data.
MEXP_LEGACY_OLDBIE.notes: Typically stored in character data structures like 'pcdata->mud_exp', this constant is used in input handling and character loading routines to set or check the experience type.
MEXP_LEGACY_OLDBIE.rationale: This constant exists to support the differentiation of legacy experience profiles from newer types, enabling the system to apply appropriate logic and compatibility measures for older characters.
MEXP_LEGACY_OLDBIE.name: MEXP_LEGACY_OLDBIE

usages.save_8cc, bool load_char_obj(Descriptor: Set as the default experience type during character creation.
usages.structconn_1_1_get_mud_exp_state, State: Constant value assigned to ch->pcdata->mud_exp when the user selects option '3'.
